annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: states that there is no non-vanishing continuous tangent vector field on even-dimensional

annotations.note: Grep across Mathlib for 'hairy', tangent vector fields on spheres, and non-vanishing sections finds no formalization of the hairy ball theorem.

annotations.label: Sum of indices equals Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: the sum of all of the indices at all of the zeros must be two

annotations.note: No Poincaré–Hopf-style result exists in Mathlib; only an algebraic/chain-complex Euler characteristic appears (Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/EulerCharacteristic.lean).

annotations.label: Vector field zero on compact 2-manifolds with nonzero Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: for any compact regular 2-dimensional manifold with non-zero Euler characteristic, any continuous tangent vector field has at least one zero

annotations.note: No formalization of vector-field zeros on compact 2-manifolds via Euler characteristic is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal vector generation in computer graphics

annotations.note: An application-flavored example with no corresponding Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Lefschetz argument for hairy ball

anchor.snippet: the Lefschetz number (total trace on homology ) of the identity mapping is 2

annotations.note: Grep for 'Lefschetz' in Mathlib turns up only Ax–Grothendieck-style results, no Lefschetz fixed-point/number theory.

annotations.label: Fixed point or antipodal point corollary

anchor.snippet: any continuous function that maps an even-dimensional sphere into itself has either a fixed point or a point that maps onto its own antipodal point

annotations.note: No fixed-point/antipodal corollary on even-dimensional spheres is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fixed-point property of even-dimensional projective space

anchor.snippet: any even-dimensional projective space has the fixed-point property

annotations.note: Grep finds no 'fixed-point property' for projective spaces in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: the 2 n -sphere has no non-vanishing vector field for

annotations.note: The higher-dimensional analogue is also absent from Mathlib (same grep as the base case).

annotations.label: Non-vanishing field on odd-dimensional spheres

anchor.snippet: an odd-dimensional sphere admits a non-vanishing tangent vector field through a simple process of considering coordinates of the ambient even-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: No construction of a non-vanishing tangent vector field on odd-dimensional spheres is formalized in Mathlib's sphere infrastructure (Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Instances/Sphere.lean).

annotations.label: Rotating rigid ball velocity field

anchor.snippet: rotation of a rigid ball around its fixed axis gives rise to a continuous tangential vector field of velocities

annotations.note: Physical illustration, not a formal mathematical statement; no corresponding Mathlib decl.

annotations.label: Electromagnetic wave-front application

anchor.snippet: the analysis of the propagation of electromagnetic waves , in the case when the wave-front forms a surface

annotations.note: Physics application of the theorem with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Cyclone existence on Earth

anchor.snippet: given any wind at all on the surface of the Earth, there must at all times be a cyclone somewhere

annotations.note: Informal meteorological consequence of the unformalized hairy ball theorem.
